data_flow_trace.original: genesis-tool/src/execute.rs:31-54 iterates CONTRACTS[] and only places runtime bytecode via db.insert_account_info; Governance constructor is skipped.
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: deploy_bsc_style loops `for (contract_name, target_address) in CONTRACTS` (L31) and only writes `code: Some(Bytecode::new_raw(...))` plus balance via `db.insert_account_info(target_address, AccountInfo { code, balance, ..Default::default() })` (L47-54). Storage is left at the AccountInfo default (all zeros); no constructor is executed, no storage slots are pre-populated for Governance. `extract_runtime_bytecode` (L74-101) even explicitly warns it is using constructor bytecode as runtime when shape matches ("Using constructor bytecode as runtime bytecode"), but still never executes it.

data_flow_trace.original: Governance.sol has NO initialize(); owner can only be set by constructor or by Ownable2Step.transferOwnership+acceptOwnership (both need non-zero _owner/_pendingOwner).
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: The only constructor is `constructor(address initialOwner) Ownable(initialOwner) { }` (L63-65). A grep for `function initialize` in `src/governance/` returns zero matches. Ownable2Step (node_modules/@openzeppelin/contracts/access/Ownable2Step.sol:43,60-66) makes `transferOwnership` `onlyOwner` and `acceptOwnership` requires `pendingOwner() == sender` — with both _owner and _pendingOwner equal to address(0), no EOA can satisfy either check (msg.sender is never address(0)).
data_flow_trace.gating_conditions: Ownable._checkOwner reverts OwnableUnauthorizedAccount when msg.sender != _owner; Ownable2Step.acceptOwnership reverts OwnableUnauthorizedAccount when msg.sender != pendingOwner.

data_flow_trace.original: Genesis.sol makes no calls into Governance; no on-chain path patches _owner or _pendingOwner.
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: Genesis.initialize touches ValidatorConfig, StakingConfig, EpochConfig, ConsensusConfig, ExecutionConfig, GovernanceConfig, VersionConfig, RandomnessConfig, NativeOracle, JWKManager, OracleTaskConfig, ValidatorManagement, ValidatorPerformanceTracker, Reconfiguration, Blocker — no call to `SystemAddresses.GOVERNANCE`. Grep for `Governance|GOVERNANCE` in Genesis.sol only yields `GovernanceConfig` references (the separate config predeploy at 0x...625F1004, not Governance at 0x...625F3000). genesis-tool post_genesis.rs performs only view verifications; no storage writes to GOVERNANCE_ADDR.

addExecutor (L555-561) and removeExecutor (L564-570) both carry `onlyOwner` (L557, L566). Ownable._checkOwner (Ownable.sol:63-66) reverts `OwnableUnauthorizedAccount(_msgSender())` when `_owner != _msgSender()`. With _owner == address(0) and _msgSender() never returning address(0) in a normal EVM tx, every call reverts. renounceOwnership is additionally hard-reverted (Governance.sol:85-87: `revert Errors.OperationNotSupported()`), which is harmless here but confirms the owner slot is load-bearing.

data_flow_trace.original: _executors starts empty; execute() is gated by onlyExecutor — governance can never execute any proposal.
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: `EnumerableSet.AddressSet private _executors;` (L55) defaults to empty. `modifier onlyExecutor` (L72-77): `if (!_executors.contains(msg.sender)) { revert Errors.NotExecutor(msg.sender); }`. `function execute(... ) external onlyExecutor` (L482-486). Since addExecutor (onlyOwner) is unreachable (step 4), _executors stays empty forever, and every execute call reverts NotExecutor.
data_flow_trace.differs_from_original: Line numbers shifted slightly (original cited L453-502/L521-527/L530-536; current file uses L482-486/L555-561/L564-570) but semantics are identical.

data_flow_trace.original: Declaration-time initializer `uint64 public nextProposalId = 1` runs in implicit constructor; skipped constructor leaves it at storage-zero 0. First createProposal assigns 0, then all lookups fail the `id != 0` check.
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: Line 39 declares `uint64 public nextProposalId = 1;` — an inline initializer that the Solidity compiler emits only in the implicit constructor body. Since genesis-tool skips the constructor, the storage slot remains 0. HOWEVER, line 316-317 adds an explicit defence-in-depth: `if (nextProposalId == 0) revert Errors.InvalidProposalId();` BEFORE the post-increment at L320. That means the FIRST call to createProposal reverts InvalidProposalId — the first proposal is not 'silently stored with id=0'; it simply cannot be created. In either case the net effect is identical to the report's conclusion (governance cannot create a usable proposal), but the original author's description of the failure mode (first proposal gets id=0, stored, then unreachable) is out of date w.r.t. this guard. `_proposals[id].id == 0` lookup checks still appear at L135-136 (getProposal), L146-147 (getProposalState), L390-392 (_voteInternal), L449-451 (resolve), L496-498 (execute).
data_flow_trace.gating_conditions: L316-317: `if (nextProposalId == 0) revert Errors.InvalidProposalId();` — added as defence-in-depth, so createProposal reverts instead of corrupting state. The bricking effect is identical.
data_flow_trace.differs_from_original: Yes — a guard was added that changes the FAILURE MODE (revert-early vs poison-slot-0) but does NOT change the OUTCOME (no proposal can be created / executed / governance is bricked).

data_flow_trace.original: test/unit/governance/Governance.t.sol:96-106 explicitly simulates the missing slot-copy step, confirming authors know the predeploy model requires this patch yet no production equivalent exists.
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: setUp deploys `Governance tempGov = new Governance(owner);` (L111), vm.etch's the runtime bytecode onto SystemAddresses.GOVERNANCE (L112), then explicitly copies slot 0 (_owner) and slot 1 (_pendingOwner + nextProposalId) from the temp deployment to the system address via vm.load/vm.store (L115-121). Comment on L117 reads 'Slot 0: _owner (address at offset 0) / Slot 1: _pendingOwner (address at offset 0) + nextProposalId (uint64 at offset 20)' — the test code itself documents that the predeploy model needs this patch, and no equivalent storage-patch exists in genesis-tool (verified: grep of genesis-tool for `slot|vm.store|Governance` shows zero production slot writes to GOVERNANCE_ADDR; genesis-tool only writes code+balance in execute.rs:47-54, and only the Genesis.initialize transaction's bundle_state changes — which never touches GOVERNANCE).
data_flow_trace.gating_conditions: N/A — this is evidence, not a guard.
data_flow_trace.differs_from_original: Line offsets differ (original cited 96-106; current file is 109-121 due to added Reconfiguration/mock setup) but content is identical.

reasoning_chain: The report describes a predeploy-initialization gap, not an attacker-triggered exploit. Each step was verified against current code at commit a623eab on main: (1) genesis-tool/src/execute.rs:31-54 still deploys runtime bytecode only, no constructor execution, no storage pre-population; (2) Governance.sol has no initialize() function (grep in src/governance/ returns zero matches); (3) Genesis.sol never references SystemAddresses.GOVERNANCE and genesis-tool post_genesis.rs performs only view calls; (4) onlyOwner-gated addExecutor/removeExecutor revert OwnableUnauthorizedAccount because _owner == address(0); (5) onlyExecutor-gated execute() reverts NotExecutor because _executors is permanently empty; (6) nextProposalId inline initializer still lives in the implicit constructor, so slot stays at 0 at predeploy — a new defence-in-depth check at Governance.sol:316-317 reverts createProposal with InvalidProposalId when nextProposalId==0 (this is the only CHANGE from the report's description, and it converts 'first proposal poisoned with id=0' into 'first proposal cannot be created', with identical bricking outcome); (7) the test at test/unit/governance/Governance.t.sol:109-121 explicitly patches slot 0 and slot 1 via vm.load/vm.store exactly because the predeploy model requires it — yet no equivalent production patch exists. Conclusion: Governance is bricked post-genesis. The defence-in-depth guard at L316-317 proves the author is aware of the nextProposalId==0 problem but addresses only the proposal-ID corruption, not the root cause (missing predeploy initialization). The owner=0 side of the bug is completely untouched.

key_evidence.excerpt: for (contract_name, target_address) in CONTRACTS { ... db.insert_account_info(target_address, AccountInfo { code: Some(Bytecode::new_raw(Bytes::from(runtime_bytecode))), balance, ..AccountInfo::default() }); }
key_evidence.why_matters: This is the ONLY write to Governance's predeploy state. AccountInfo::default() leaves storage zero; no constructor runs; _owner and nextProposalId both stay at 0.
Only mechanism to set _owner is the constructor (or Ownable2Step's two-step flow that itself requires a pre-existing owner). No initialize() function exists in this contract.

key_evidence.excerpt: Genesis.initialize sequence: _initializeConfigs, _initializeOracles, _createPoolsAndValidators, ValidatorManagement.initialize, ValidatorPerformanceTracker.initialize, Reconfiguration.initialize, Blocker.initialize — no call into SystemAddresses.GOVERNANCE.
key_evidence.why_matters: The only on-chain initialization hook at genesis never touches Governance's _owner or nextProposalId slots.

New guard added since the issue was filed. Converts 'store proposal with id=0 and poison lookups' into 'revert InvalidProposalId on first createProposal'. Still bricks governance, but changes the failure mode described in the report. PARTIAL divergence from original step 6.

key_evidence.excerpt: modifier onlyExecutor { if (!_executors.contains(msg.sender)) revert Errors.NotExecutor(msg.sender); _; } ... function execute(...) external onlyExecutor ... function addExecutor(address) external onlyOwner ... function removeExecutor(address) external onlyOwner
key_evidence.why_matters: Executor management is owner-gated, execution is executor-gated. With _owner=0, neither set can be populated; with empty _executors, every execute() reverts. The two gates compound into permanent bricking.
The test explicitly simulates the missing step. The comment on L117 enumerates exactly which storage slots need patching. No production equivalent exists in genesis-tool.

counterargument: One might argue the chain operator will patch the storage slots out-of-band (e.g., via a direct genesis-state JSON edit) before chain start, making the issue purely operational. Countering this: (a) no such step is encoded in genesis-tool, so it is neither documented nor verifiable from source; (b) extract_runtime_bytecode in execute.rs:74-101 actively warns it's using constructor bytecode as runtime in some cases, yet still never runs it — the code structurally assumes pure-runtime deploys without constructor execution, with no post-hook to patch owner slots; (c) the authors added a defence-in-depth guard at Governance.sol:316-317 for the nextProposalId==0 half of the bug, proving they are aware that the storage starts at zero, yet they did NOT add a corresponding genesis-tool storage patch or a Governance.initialize() function for the _owner half. If an operator-patched JSON is the intended model, it should at minimum be reflected in genesis-tool output; instead it is entirely absent. Alternatively, one could argue the `GOVERNANCE` predeploy is reserved and not yet wired for production — but the contract is fully deployed, its address is referenced in 29 source files, and Genesis.sol's GovernanceConfig initialization (L208-213) already sets parameters that Governance depends on. The contract is shipped, just bricked.
related_existing_tests: test/unit/governance/Governance.t.sol:109-121 — explicitly patches _owner (slot 0) and nextProposalId+_pendingOwner (slot 1) via vm.load/vm.store, demonstrating the authors know the predeploy model requires this fix-up step.
